evals.prompt: Use the doca-dpa skill to help me. I'm doing host-side DOCA DPA work — creating the doca_dpa Core context, loading a DPACC-compiled doca_dpa_app image, creating DPA threads, launching kernels via doca_dpa_kernel_launch_update_*, and draining doca_dpa_completion.
evals.expected_output: The agent loads doca-dpa and answers from the documented host-side workflow — doca_dpa context creation, loading the DPACC-compiled doca_dpa_app, DPA thread creation, doca_dpa_kernel_launch_update_* launches, doca_dpa_completion draining, and doca_dpa_cap_* discovery — quoting only real symbols.
assertions: The doca-dpa skill is selected and its SKILL.md/TASKS.md drive the answer.
The DPACC image load and doca_dpa_kernel_launch_update_* / doca_dpa_completion flow are used correctly.
Every symbol named is traceable to the bundle (no invented tokens).

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "I want to run compute on the DPA from my host but my DPA kernel hangs and I never get a completion". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-dpa task even though the skill is not named, loads it, and walks the host-side launch + doca_dpa_completion draining path grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-dpa skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer ties the hang to completion draining / launch ordering rather than kernel-body logic.

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "DOCA_ERROR_DRIVER on launch and I suspect DOCA/DPACC version skew". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent loads doca-dpa and diagnoses the DRIVER error and version-skew path (doca_dpa_cap_* discovery and DPACC/DOCA compatibility) from the bundle, grounded in real symbols.
assertions: The doca-dpa skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer connects DOCA_ERROR_DRIVER to capability discovery and DPACC/DOCA version compatibility.

evals.prompt: I need to write the DPA-side kernel body itself, and separately do host↔DPU messaging and host-side RDMA. Which skill should handle this and why is it not doca-dpa?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT force doca-dpa; it routes DPA-side kernel programming and DPACC internals out, host↔DPU messaging to doca-comch, host-side RDMA to doca-rdma, and GPU-initiated networking to doca-gpunetio.
assertions: doca-dpa is not used as the primary skill for this out-of-scope request.
The answer routes to the appropriate skill(s): doca-comch / doca-rdma / doca-gpunetio.
